block.translate: блок, блокировка, преграда, квартал, кубики, блокировать, препятствовать
mnemonic: block [блок] квартал, плита, пробка - (Блок) НАТО уже, всего лишь, в квартале от России.

flock [flɔk]
lock [lɒk] - зам- стадо, толпа ок, запирать
block [blɒk] - квартал, блок, блокировать
***
Деньги стекаются "flock" -
я их под замок "lock".

examples.eng: They live in this block.
He lives in the next block.
Please don't block the doorway.
They went around the block.
The garage was made of concrete blocks.
examples.ru: Гараж был построен из бетонных блоков.

examples.eng: The sink's blocked up.
Hats cleaned and blocked.
examples.ru: Шляпы вычищены и надеты на болванку.

examples.eng: You'd better block up that hole in the wall., it's letting the cold in.
examples.ru: Заделай-ка дыру в стене, оттуда дует.

examples.eng: She lived on our block.
block the wheels of a car
Let's walk round the block.
an easy walk around the block
Can you move? You're blocking my light.
examples.ru: Можешь отойти? Ты мне свет загораживаешь.

examples.eng: The office block looks as if it's just been chucked up.
Her arteries are blocked.
His vote blocked the treaty.
The Liberals vote as a block
The church is down the block.
Snowdrifts blocked our progress.
An accident was blocking traffic.
A fallen tree is blocking the road.
a wall built out of concrete blocks
The clouds have blocked out the sun.
They live just up the block from us.
examples.ru: Они живут всего в квартале от нас.

examples.eng: His brother blocked him at every turn.
examples.ru: Брат мешал ему на каждом шагу.

examples.eng: There's an accident blocking the road.
The building occupies an entire city block.
The Senate blocked publication of the report.
sculpt a swan out of a block of ice
the ruins of a bombed-out office block
